subject: process_model
what: no persistent CLI processes; each turn is a fresh shell-out
why: 2026-04-05 spike showed all three CLIs expose non-interactive JSONL mode with session resumption; pty/tmux was dead code

subject: concurrency
what: one in-flight turn per conversation, FIFO queue
why: CLIs are single-threaded interactive processes; principle #1 seam requires serialization above the adapter

subject: filesystem
what: each CLI turn runs in an ephemeral scratch cwd; no access to user files
why: OpenAI chat API has no cwd concept; only coherent interpretation is to suppress CLI native file tools at the sandbox

subject: claude_exit_code
text: claude -p exits 0 even when a rate_limit_event with status != allowed was emitted. Never infer health from exit code; always inspect the JSONL event stream for rate_limit_event and result.subtype.

subject: cold_cache_tax
text: Cold CLI invocation eats 6k (gemini) to 14k (codex) input tokens of agent-prompt overhead before the user's prompt is even processed. claude's prompt-cache window is 1h. If jRouter idles >1h, first turn back pays the full tax. Warm-cache discipline is a routing concern, not just an optimization.

subject: agent_contamination
text: Under OAuth subscription auth, the backend CLIs run as tool-wielding agents framed by their own system prompts. A trivial prompt ('reply ok') produced num_turns:3 in the spike — internal agent work the client didn't request. This is observable (log num_turns and tool events per turn) but not preventable. Design for it, don't fight it.

subject: gemini_compound
text: Gemini auto-routes across sub-models within a single turn (spike saw gemini-3-flash-preview and gemini-2.5-flash-lite in the same call). stats.models has the per-model breakdown. GeminiAdapter must surface this as a compound-provider quota event, not a single counter.

subject: session_id_shapes
text: Three vendors, three session id shapes: claude accepts a client-chosen UUID via --session-id; gemini returns a server-assigned index in its init event; codex returns a server-assigned thread_id in thread.started. The adapter boundary must normalize this — the router stores an opaque string and never parses it.

goal: scaffold the orientation loop (STATUS, JOURNAL, CLAUDE.md, scripts/)

text: canonical project name is FreelOAder (package: freeloader); old 'jRouter'/'jrouter' references in non-journal files have been renamed
subject: project_name
why: repo dir, PLAN.md, README.md all use freeloader; the jrouter references in STATUS/ROADMAP/AGENT/CLAUDE/scripts were drift from a prior name and would break every gate on the first commit that creates src/

text: CLIAdapter.send() Protocol carries system: str | None = None from day one
subject: system_prompt_slot
why: phase-5 tool-call shim requires adapter-owned system-prompt slot (PLAN.md § 'Things to explicitly *not* do'); retrofitting the signature after ClaudeAdapter/GeminiAdapter/CodexAdapter exist is a three-way rewrite plus three contract-test rebaselines

text: chat-completions history diff lives in src/freeloader/canonical/history_diff.py — not the frontend, not the adapter
subject: history_diff_home
why: principle #6 says handlers are ~50 lines; decision #3 says adapters are text-in/text-out. Client edits/regenerations/reordered system messages need a real home. Canonical layer is the only coherent owner
source: PLAN.md principle #4, decision recorded in phase-1 exit criteria

text: Anthropic ToS crackdown (Jan 9, 2026 server-side block; Feb 19 policy; Apr 4 OpenClaw ban) prohibits OAuth tokens from Free/Pro/Max accounts being used in 'any other product, tool, or service' including the Agent SDK. Enforcement was server-side client-identity validation rejecting spoofed Claude Code headers — the OpenClaw-style pattern of extracting the OAuth token and calling api.anthropic.com directly. BUT Anthropic staff clarified that running the genuine claude binary as a subprocess is still allowed ('even on a remote server, even over SSH'); CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN is officially documented for CI/scripts. FreelOAder's MVP path (shell-out to claude -p --output-format stream-json) is the sanctioned pattern, not the banned one. --bare OAuth path is dead (confirmed). Remaining risks: (a) the written ToS is stricter than the staff clarification; (b) commercial/shared use requires API keys; (c) cold-cache tax >1h is the behavioral signature Anthropic flags wrappers by — warm-cache discipline per session_id is a ToS concern, not just cost.
subject: anthropic_3p_ban

text: phase 1 step 1.0 scaffolding complete — all loop artifacts + scripts committed in 8e01c36..17dd2dd; stale 'first commit' checkbox in prior STATUS was an artifact of the cold-start procedure

insert step 1.0.5 (architecture review) between 1.0 and 1.1 — adversarial review of PLAN.md + ROADMAP.md before first code lands; chosen as a step rather than a full phase to avoid re-litigating settled ground and to match the loop shape (AGENT.md § scope discipline)
subject: architecture_review_step
why: PLAN.md encodes 8 principles + 11 decisions + spike findings; after step 1.1 those harden into code. Cost of revising a PLAN.md line now is an edit; cost after src/freeloader/adapters/claude.py exists is a refactor plus test rebaseline. One-shot review step with JOURNAL entries as output — no new gate script, no new phase.

text: architecture review — adversarial pass over PLAN.md + ROADMAP.md before first code lands
Delta is a tagged union (TextDelta | SessionIdDelta | UsageDelta | RateLimitDelta); frontend pattern-matches per variant
subject: delta_tagged_union
why: flat Delta with 'last one has session id' semantics conflates content stream and control channel. Every adapter interleaves text, session id, usage, and rate-limit events on a single JSONL stream; variants must be distinguishable at every yield. PLAN.md principle #1 revised.

text: drop 'logged' as a separate terminal state; journal write is atomic with reaching a terminal state (complete / cancelled / backend_error / rate_limited / timed_out)
subject: turn_state_simplified
why: a 'drained but not yet logged' window creates a consistency gap — if the process crashes there, conversation history and JOURNAL disagree on the outcome. Journal-write failures surface as adapter_error. PLAN.md principle #2 revised.

text: history_diff MVP scope frozen at three cases (append, last-assistant regeneration, mismatch→raise); mid-history edits raise 400
subject: history_diff_mvp_scope
why: the fourth case (truncate stored history at divergence, replay from N onward) is cheap to add once the three-case spine works. Pre-building it is scope creep per AGENT.md § scope discipline. Revisit in phase 5 if a real client surfaces the need.

text: two logs, one format: global JOURNAL.jsonl for all events (build-time + per-turn + quota signals); per-conversation <data_dir>/<conversation_id>.jsonl for canonical messages only (replay on rebind). Router reads JOURNAL.
subject: storage_layout
why: principle #5 says quota is an event stream the router consumes; scanning N per-conversation files per routing decision doesn't scale. Separating events (global) from messages (per-conversation) matches the two access patterns. PLAN.md decision #6 revised.

text: canonical replay into a new backend contains only user messages and final-assistant-message turns; intermediate agent contamination preserved in metadata but not replayed
subject: replay_scope
why: OpenAI chat surface only exposes user + assistant roles. claude's num_turns>1 from a single client turn (internal tool work) was never part of the conversation the client thinks it's having. Lossy for the backend reasoning trace; faithful to the client-visible conversation. PLAN.md principle #3 revised.

text: no health() on CLIAdapter Protocol in MVP; phase 4 may add probe_quota() only if routing needs a decision before the first turn fires
subject: health_probe_deferred
why: principle #5 says quota is an event stream, not a probe. health() with ambiguous QuotaState return was conflating liveness (the subprocess) with quota (the subscription). Subprocess liveness is measured per-turn (did it spawn, did it exit with result). Remove now; retrofit cost after phase-1 code lands is one-time. PLAN.md principle #1 revised.

text: HTTP framework: FastAPI (async-native, first-class SSE via StreamingResponse, built-in OpenAPI docs, Pydantic request validation matches OpenAI JSON Schema error shapes)
subject: http_framework
why: previously only shown in README architecture diagram, never formalized as a decision — gap would let step 1.1 accidentally pick Starlette/Hypercorn. Every OpenAI-compatible proxy example uses FastAPI; client error shapes stay familiar. New PLAN.md decision #12.

text: runtime logging = stdlib logging; architectural events = JOURNAL.jsonl
subject: runtime_logging
why: two audiences, two destinations. stdlib logging (default WARNING, INFO with FREELOADER_LOG_LEVEL=info) for server-runtime diagnostics (port binding, spawn failures, stacktraces, request IDs). JOURNAL.jsonl for per-turn records + quota + decisions + phase transitions. Different retention, volume, consumers. No structlog/loguru — stdlib can emit JSON via formatter. New PLAN.md decision #13.

text: add three scope excluders: no static type checker in gate, no CI/pre-commit, no pytest plugins beyond pytest itself (pytest-asyncio likely exception, decide at step 1.1)
subject: scope_excluders_added
why: personal-use prototype ~500 lines; mypy/pyright fights Protocol + asyncio + duck-typed adapters for little gain until phase 3. GitHub Actions is overhead the MVP never pays back. Pytest plugins are the usual scope-creep vector. PLAN.md § 'Things to explicitly not do' updated.

text: concurrent JOURNAL.jsonl appends within the server process need a single-writer asyncio lock — POSIX O_APPEND is atomic only under PIPE_BUF (~4KB) and a long assistant message in a turn_done record can exceed that. scripts/reflect.sh is single-process so unaffected. Becomes relevant in phase 3+ when multiple turns run concurrently across conversations.
subject: journal_append_atomicity

text: claude's rate_limit_event has no counterpart 'allowed again' event — once status != 'allowed' the router switches and never switches back without explicit re-enable. resetsAt (unix timestamp) is the signal; phase 4 routing needs a scheduled re-enable (e.g., an asyncio.call_later firing at resetsAt or a polled probe). Without this, the first rate limit makes a backend permanently unreachable for the process lifetime.
subject: rate_limit_stickiness

text: architecture review complete — 13 findings, 7 PLAN.md revisions, 9 decisions + 2 lessons in journal, 1 deferred (/codex consult to step 1.1/1.2)
phase-3 adapter implementation order: codex first, gemini second (inside phase 3; phase-1 claude unchanged)
subject: phase3_adapter_order
why: codex is structurally closer to claude — clean JSONL event stream (thread.started / turn.started / item.completed / turn.completed), single-session-per-invocation, server-assigned thread_id string. Gemini is the outlier: compound provider with per-sub-model stats.models breakdown, server-assigned integer index as session id, ambiguous system-prompt injection. Doing codex second exposes what pluralizing the Protocol requires on a low-variance shape before gemini's compound-provider quirk lands. If the Protocol needs revision when the second adapter lands, doing that revision against codex (similar shape to claude) is cheaper than against gemini.
source: ROADMAP.md § phase 3, PLAN.md § MVP step 3, README.md § stack

text: delete 'persistent authorized subprocess with stdin/stdout bridged to FreelOAder' text from PLAN.md component description; replace with fresh-subprocess-per-turn wording consistent with spike findings
subject: persistent_subprocess_text_removed
why: stale text from pre-spike draft contradicted the rest of PLAN.md (capability matrix, principle #2, MVP step 1). First implementer would have built process-handle abstractions instead of per-turn shell-out. Codex P1 finding from /codex consult 1.0.5 pass.

text: chat completions conversation identity: SHA-256 hash of message prefix (system messages + first user message), with X-FreelOAder-Conversation-Id header override. Prefix-hash divergence → new conversation, no fuzzy match / LRU recovery
subject: conversation_identity
why: OpenAI /v1/chat/completions has no conversation_id concept. Without a defined identity strategy, 'diff against stored conversation' (principle #4, phase 1 exit) has no defined lookup key. Hash-of-prefix handles well-behaved clients that resend history verbatim (90% case); optional header handles power users wanting stable ids (10% case). Codex P1 finding from /codex consult 1.0.5 pass.

text: Responses API: FreelOAder-owned opaque response_id (fr_resp_<uuid>); internally maps to (conversation_id, turn_id, backend_session_id at that turn). Backend identity never leaks into OpenAI-facing fields
subject: response_id_ownership
why: original 'previous_response_id maps directly to backend session id' leaked backend identity into the OpenAI API surface, would break on provider switch / cancellation / retry, and couples phase-3 routing to phase-1 response semantics. FreelOAder-owned id namespace with internal mapping survives provider switches. Codex P1 finding from /codex consult 1.0.5 pass.

text: filesystem sandbox is defense-in-depth via CLI --add-dir/-C flags, NOT a hard boundary. OS-level isolation (sandbox-exec / container) deferred to phase 3+ if threat model demands it
subject: filesystem_sandbox_scope
why: ROADMAP phase 1 exit originally claimed 'backend cannot read project files' — stronger than the CLI flags actually enforce. --add-dir grants directory access but doesn't deny paths like ~/.ssh, ~/.config, repo parent dirs. Weakening the claim matches reality; strengthening via OS sandbox is real work deferred until it's justified. Codex P1 finding from /codex consult 1.0.5 pass.

text: expand Delta union: add FinishDelta, ErrorDelta, RawDelta. Full union = TextDelta | FinishDelta | SessionIdDelta | UsageDelta | RateLimitDelta | ErrorDelta | RawDelta. FinishDelta carries finish_reason (stop/length/content_filter/tool_calls); ErrorDelta terminates stream; RawDelta is the vendor-event escape hatch
subject: delta_union_expanded
why: original 4-variant union (Text|SessionId|Usage|RateLimit) had no representation for OpenAI finish_reason, adapter-observed errors, or vendor events we haven't canonicalized. Frontend would have accumulated special cases. Adding FinishDelta/ErrorDelta/RawDelta now (7 variants) is cheaper than retrofitting after three adapters exist. Codex P1 finding from /codex consult 1.0.5 pass.

text: split storage into three append-only JSONL logs: repo-root JOURNAL.jsonl (build-time only, tracked in git); <data_dir>/events.jsonl (runtime events, untracked); <data_dir>/conversations/<id>.jsonl (canonical messages, untracked). Router reads events.jsonl, not JOURNAL
subject: three_log_split
why: previous 'two logs' revision (decision #6 after initial 1.0.5 pass) put runtime events in repo-root JOURNAL.jsonl — running the server would dirty the repo forever, leak conversation metadata into git, and break the append-only-vs-HEAD~1 gate check. Three-log split isolates build-time (git-tracked) from runtime (data_dir). Revises decision #6 for the second time in step 1.0.5. Codex P2 finding from /codex consult 1.0.5 pass.

text: retry only before session_id or text output is observed; post-observation retries must start a fresh backend session (no -r) and flag prior attempt 'ambiguous' in runtime event log. Router treats 'ambiguous' as stronger evidence of unhealth than clean failure
subject: retry_idempotency
why: original decision #8 allowed 'retry once against the same backend' without distinguishing pre- vs post-observation. If CLI assigned session id or emitted partial text, retrying with -r forks or duplicates turn state. Duplicated user turns silently corrupt conversation history. Codex P2 finding from /codex consult 1.0.5 pass.

text: gate_common.sh invokes ruff/pytest via 'uv run' rather than PATH lookup. Bootstrap python3 check for JOURNAL validity stays as system python3 (runs before pyproject.toml exists)
subject: gate_uv_wrapper
why: user's ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md enforces uv-first for all project Python tooling. Direct 'ruff' / 'pytest' invocation in gate_common relied on global ruff/pytest on PATH, which isn't guaranteed in a clean checkout. 'uv run' resolves to the project's locked toolchain. Codex P2 finding from /codex consult 1.0.5 pass.

text: OpenAI request fields beyond messages/stream/model are not yet decided: temperature, max_tokens, stop, response_format, tool_choice, multimodal blocks, stream_options, parallel_tool_calls, metadata. Silent ignore creates hard-to-debug client behavior (client sets temperature, FreelOAder drops it, output looks wrong but no error). Phase 2 decision: enumerate which fields are honored / ignored-with-warning / rejected. Codex P3 finding from /codex consult 1.0.5 pass.
subject: openai_field_passthrough

text: architecture review round 2 complete (/codex consult) — 10 findings, 6 PLAN revisions, 3 ROADMAP revisions, 1 script revision, 8 decisions, 1 lesson. Step 1.0.5 totals: 23 findings, 17 decisions, 3 lessons across eng-manager + /codex passes

history replay format for first turn after rebind = role-tagged plaintext ([SYSTEM]/[USER]/[ASSISTANT] delimiters flatten canonical turns into single prompt). Adapters may pick different delimiter tokens per vendor tokenizer bias. Lossy vs native multi-turn; only used on first turn after rebind
subject: history_replay_format
why: non-interactive CLIs take a single text prompt; replay without role delimiters collapses boundaries and lets prior assistant text become prompt-injection fuel. PLAN principle #3 previously hand-waved 'replay full history into the first turn's prompt' without specifying format. Gemini P1 finding from round-3 review.

text: cancellation DISCARDS backend_session_id (previously 'preserve if backend reported one'). SIGTERMed CLI leaves partial assistant output in its local state; resuming via the poisoned id causes permanent state drift (backend sees User → partial assistant → User retry while canonical shows User → User retry). Next turn starts fresh session with full canonical replay
subject: cancellation_discards_session
why: decision #5 originally preserved backend_session_id on cancellation for 'next turn can still resume'. Gemini round-3 flagged that this guarantees state drift between backend and canonical history after any cancelled turn. Cost of fresh-session replay is one cold cache tax; cost of state drift is silent corruption of all future turns in that conversation.

text: CLI-side state isolated per conversation via env-var redirection (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, CODEX_HOME, XDG_CONFIG_HOME+XDG_DATA_HOME for gemini) pointing to <data_dir>/cli-state/<conversation_id>/. OAuth credentials inherited from user global state; only session/cache/history dirs are redirected. On restart, all per-conversation state dirs torn down. Fallback for no-env-var CLIs: per-provider mutex serializing turns on that provider
subject: cli_state_isolation
why: decision #3 sandboxed cwd but CLIs persist session state in global dirs (~/.claude/, ~/.codex/, ~/.config/gemini/). Concurrent turns across conversations race on gemini -r latest, session index assignment, cross-context leakage. Gemini P1 round-3 finding.

text: codex --ephemeral is NOT used; codex uses 'exec resume <thread_id>' for turn continuation, matching claude's -r pattern. --ephemeral contradicts the resume path: no on-disk state means no thread to resume on turn 2. CLI state cleanup handled via per-conversation state dirs (decision #16), not --ephemeral
subject: codex_uses_resume_not_ephemeral
why: ROADMAP phase 3 originally said 'Codex --ephemeral mode is used' alongside 'resume via session id on subsequent turns' — mutually exclusive. Per-conversation state dirs + torn-down on restart gives the same no-persistence-across-restarts property without breaking resume. Gemini P1 round-3 finding.

text: UsageDelta schema always keyed by sub-model: UsageDelta.models = dict[str, ModelUsage] where ModelUsage = {input_tokens, output_tokens, cached_input_tokens}. Single-model providers (claude, codex) report one entry; compound providers (gemini) report multiple entries per turn matching stats.models breakdown. Top-level totals are derived (sum)
subject: usage_delta_schema
why: UsageDelta was previously under-specified as 'per-turn token usage' — shape didn't require the models dict. Gemini auto-routes across sub-models within a single turn (spike: gemini-3-flash-preview + gemini-2.5-flash-lite in one call); flat {input, output} discards the per-sub-model breakdown and makes quota tracking per sub-model impossible. Gemini P2 round-3 finding, refines the earlier gemini_compound lesson (JOURNAL line 8).

text: router derived view over <data_dir>/events.jsonl must be maintained IN-MEMORY and updated incrementally on each event append — NOT re-scanned from file on every routing decision. File is durability; memory is operational. Scan cost grows unbounded with uptime; at 10k turns/day, a week of events is 70k lines / ~10MB — fine for personal use now, pathological at scale. Phase 4 implementation concern
subject: events_jsonl_derived_view

text: codex emits 'item.completed' events, not incremental text deltas. CodexAdapter will yield exactly one TextDelta at turn end (not token-by-token). /v1/chat/completions with stream=true against codex-backed conversation will look like a hang-then-burst to clients expecting token streaming. Two options: (a) document honestly (README + server startup log), (b) investigate whether codex has an undocumented sub-item text streaming event. Phase 2 concern for stream-parity across adapters
subject: codex_streaming_illusion

text: architecture review round 3 (gemini) complete — 7 findings, 3 PLAN revisions + 1 new decision + 1 ROADMAP revision, 5 decisions, 2 lessons. Step 1.0.5 cumulative totals across all three rounds: 30 findings, 22 decisions, 5 lessons

review rounds past 1-2 produce speculative decisions. reviewers told to find issues will find issues; non-overlapping findings across rounds is evidence of pattern-matching, not discovery. decisions #14/#15/#16 (added in rounds 2-3 of 1.0.5) demoted to provisional: they fix problems reviewers imagined, not code that hit them. smell test: can step 1.1 proceed without these? yes -> they were speculative. future rule: a pre-code decision needs 'what bugged code am i trying to avoid' justification, not just 'a reviewer raised it'.
subject: review_scope_creep

text: 1.1 scaffold pyproject.toml + src/freeloader/ skeleton
goal: green common-invariants + file-existence slice of gate_1

text: pyproject dev-group = ruff + pytest + pytest-asyncio; no runtime deps in 1.1
subject: step_1_1_scope
why: FastAPI/httpx materialize when imports exist (1.2+); deps-without-use is premature

text: pytest-asyncio asyncio_mode=auto (vs explicit @pytest.mark.asyncio)
subject: pytest_asyncio_mode
why: subprocess adapters (1.2) + SSE handlers (phase 2) are uniformly async; auto mode avoids boilerplate on every test

text: ruff select=F,E,W,I,B,UP,SIM,C4; ignore=E501
subject: ruff_rule_set
why: formatter handles line length; rest is the defensible baseline for a young codebase without over-constraining it

text: 1.1 scaffold
outcome: common-invariants green, file-existence slice of gate_1 green, behavior slice red as expected

text: 1.2 ClaudeAdapter subprocess spawn + JSONL→Delta + golden replay

Delta union implemented as pydantic BaseModel variants with a Literal kind discriminator; one module src/freeloader/canonical/deltas.py. Picked pydantic over @dataclass because FastAPI (decision #12) pulls it in at 1.3 anyway and reusing one schema layer keeps validation+serialization uniform. Adding ~4MB of transitive deps (pydantic+pydantic_core+annotated_types+typing_inspection) is the cost.
subject: delta_types_pydantic

text: ClaudeAdapter.send() takes prompt:str (not list[CanonicalMessage]) for step 1.2. PLAN principle #1 specifies list[CanonicalMessage]+system, but no caller exists until 1.3/1.4 and CanonicalMessage lands with history_diff at 1.4. Signature will grow to match the Protocol before frontend calls through. Dead-slot speculation ruled out per AGENT.md scope rule.
subject: adapter_prompt_signature

text: JSONL→Delta split into pure map_event(event)->list[Delta] and async parse_stream(lines). Golden test exercises map_event/parse_stream directly; subprocess wiring in ClaudeAdapter.send() stays untested until 1.7 e2e. Keeps the principle #8 coverage fast and deterministic while avoiding a live claude dependency in CI.
subject: parser_subprocess_split

text: claude modelUsage field names (inputTokens/outputTokens/cacheCreationInputTokens/cacheReadInputTokens) are from the 2026-04-05 spike output and not documented. Adapter accepts both camelCase (modelUsage) and snake_case (top-level usage) to cover build churn. Confirm against live output at step 1.7 e2e; revisit _usage_from_claude_stats if shapes drift.
subject: claude_model_usage_fields

text: 1.2 ClaudeAdapter subprocess spawn + JSONL→Delta + golden replay
outcome: 7/7 golden tests + 2 scaffold tests green; gate 1: 6/10 phase checks green (file existence + golden replay); 4 behavior tests remain for 1.3-1.7

text: 1.3 FastAPI frontend: /v1/chat/completions (non-streaming) + minimal router

FastAPI extra=allow on request models — clients send tools/temperature/etc without 422. Frontend silently ignores unknown fields; formal tools=[...] stripping with warning lands in 1.6. Strict validation at the boundary is OpenAI's job, not ours, for the MVP.
subject: request_extra_allow

text: Router is one module (router.py) with a single Router class — no plugin system, no strategy pattern. Phase-1 dispatch is always ClaudeAdapter. Round-robin + quota-aware routing (principle #5) lands in phase 3/4 by growing Router, not by replacing it. PLAN § Things-not-to-do: no plugin system.
subject: router_shape

text: flatten_messages lives in adapters/claude.py for 1.3 — decision #3's role-tagged format with uppercase role tags. Single-adapter phase; pulling flatten into canonical/ with a per-adapter delimiter knob is speculative abstraction. Revisit when CodexAdapter/GeminiAdapter arrive at phase 3 and the second flatten implementation surfaces the true seam.
subject: flatten_location

text: 1.3 FastAPI frontend + minimal router + flatten_messages
outcome: 13/13 tests green (9 adapter + 4 frontend + 2 scaffold — scaffold counted once); fastapi+httpx added; gate 1: 6/10 phase checks green (infrastructure step, no new gated behavior test)

text: 1.4 history_diff + CanonicalMessage (principle #4)

history_diff returns DiffResult {action, new_messages} instead of a plain list. PLAN principle #4 names 'new_turn_messages' but the regenerate case requires the caller to know whether to drop stored's tail. Tagged result avoids the caller re-inferring regenerate from length comparisons; simpler handler logic in 1.7.
subject: diff_result_shape

text: CanonicalMessage is pydantic frozen BaseModel with role+content+metadata; no content_blocks, no tool_calls. Minimal per MVP scope rule. Frozen so list/dict equality works for history_diff (hashable not required since we compare by __eq__). Grows when a real caller needs multimodal or tool_calls.
subject: canonical_message_shape

text: 1.4 does NOT migrate adapter/router/frontend to list[CanonicalMessage]. 1.3's STATUS.md floated it; 1.4 defers to 1.7 (e2e) when the frontend integration of history_diff creates a real caller. Avoids API churn without a consumer.
subject: signature_reconciliation_deferred

text: 1.4 history_diff + CanonicalMessage
outcome: 22/22 tests green (10 new history_diff); gate 1: 7/10 phase-specific green (history_diff line flipped)

text: 1.5 scratch cwd sandbox (decision #3)

Per-turn scratch cwd (ROADMAP phase 1 literal reading) at data_dir/scratch/<session_id>/<turn_id>/; short uuid turn_id suffix prevents in-flight collisions on the same session (retry-while-regenerate scenario). finally-block rmtree with ignore_errors. cli-state isolation (decision #16) remains deferred.
subject: scratch_layout

text: data_dir resolution: FREELOADER_DATA_DIR > XDG_DATA_HOME/freeloader > ~/.local/share/freeloader. Module src/freeloader/config.py — single resolve_data_dir() func. Full freeloader.toml (decision #10) lands phase 2; 1.5 needs only the data_dir path.
subject: data_dir_resolution

text: Sandbox verified by monkeypatching asyncio.create_subprocess_exec — captures argv+cwd+cwd_exists_at_spawn without running claude. Checked at test time: scratch path is a descendant of data_dir/scratch/<session_id>, exists at spawn, is removed after send() returns. No live subprocess in this test either (matching 1.2 style). Live subprocess exercise is still 1.7 e2e.
subject: sandbox_test_strategy

text: 1.5 scratch cwd sandbox
outcome: 28/28 tests green (6 new sandbox+config); gate 1: 8/10 phase-specific green (sandbox line flipped)

text: 1.6 tools-strip (chat-only mitigation)

Chat-only mode picked for phase 1 tools-handling (PLAN hard-problem #1). Strip tools/tool_choice with stdlib-logging WARNING carrying extra={dropped_fields, model, path}. Empty tools=[] is NOT a strip — prevents warning spam for clients that always send empty arrays. Shim / passthrough options remain open for phase 5; the adapter still owns its system-prompt slot so the shim path stays viable.
subject: chat_only_strip

text: 1.6 tools-strip chat-only mitigation
outcome: 32/32 tests green (4 new tools-strip); gate 1: 9/10 phase-specific green (tools-strip line flipped); only e2e line left for 1.7

text: 1.7 e2e two-turn — closes phase 1

Router.dispatch signature now (conversation_id, stored_messages, new_messages). First-turn flatten(stored+new) for full replay; subsequent-turn flatten(new) only since backend has history. Binding lives in-memory on the Router (_bindings dict). On SessionIdDelta, update binding; adapter never emits? fall back to our --session-id. events.jsonl gets turn_done on every dispatch completion.
subject: router_dispatch_shape

text: Conversation identity: X-FreelOAder-Conversation-Id header wins; fallback hash_of_prefix = SHA-256(preceding-system-msgs + first-user-msg, sort_keys=True) → cv_<16hex>. Multimodal blocks reduced to text for hash stability. Response always sets the header so clients learn what id was assigned. Stale decision #14 reminder: still provisional, verify when a real client exercises it.
subject: conversation_identity_impl

text: Storage uses NoOp defaults so non-persistence tests don't dirty disk. ConversationStore(data_dir) + EventWriter(data_dir) are the file-backed implementations; default_store() / default_events() return NoOps. create_app accepts both explicitly. No asyncio lock for 1.7 — single-test single-process. Concurrent-writer lock per decision #6 lands when a real server handles concurrent conversations.
subject: storage_defaults

text: 1.7 e2e two-turn — phase 1 closed
outcome: 35/35 tests green; gate 1 GREEN (all 10 phase-specific checks); 2-turn context verified via fake-adapter TestClient; events.jsonl + conversations/<id>.jsonl written correctly

text: phase 1 CLOSED — ClaudeAdapter non-streaming single conversation
Starlette StreamingResponse aclose() injects GeneratorExit, not asyncio.CancelledError; cancellation handlers must catch both
subject: cancellation_exception_types

text: json.dumps default separators (', ', ': ') diverge from OpenAI wire bytes; sse_encode uses compact (',', ':')
subject: sse_byte_compatibility

text: phase 2 complete
begin phase 3
codex exec resume rejects -s/sandbox flag; sandbox policy is set on first-turn exec and persists through subsequent resumes. CodexAdapter must omit -s on resume.
subject: codex_resume_no_sandbox

text: codex exec --json emits pure JSONL on stdout; chatter ('Reading additional input from stdin...', 'Shell cwd was reset to ...') goes to stderr. Adapter ignores stderr — same shape as claude. No model identifier appears in any codex event; UsageDelta uses 'codex' provider tag until the CLI surfaces sub-model identity.
subject: codex_jsonl_shape
